title: artemisia decipiens
intro: Artemisia decipiens, also known as False Wormwood, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It belongs to the same plant family as the more well-known wormwood, Artemisia absinthium. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve digestive issues due to its bitter compounds. Artemisia decipiens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat ailments such as fever, sore throats, and menstrual cramps.
